basics.name: River Chen
basics.label: Staff Software Engineer
basics.summary: Backend engineer with a decade of experience building observable, well-tested distributed systems in Rust and Go. Happiest at the intersection of platform reliability, developer ergonomics, and thoughtful documentation.

work.name: Meridian Systems
work.summary: Tech lead for the data ingestion platform processing 2B events/day. Owned the roadmap, on-call rotation, and cross-team coordination with analytics, ML, and product teams.
highlights: Cut p99 ingest latency from 4.2s to 380ms by redesigning the batching layer in Rust
Introduced a property-based test suite that caught three pre-production regressions in the first month
Mentored four engineers, two of whom were promoted within the year

work.name: Northwind Analytics
work.summary: Built and operated the customer-facing query API serving 500+ enterprise tenants.
highlights: Led the Go-to-Rust migration of the query planner, reducing memory footprint by 60%
Designed the multi-tenant rate-limiting system still in production today
Authored the on-call runbook and ran the first internal chaos-engineering day

work.name: Lumen Labs
work.summary: Full-stack engineer on a small team building observability tools for microservice deployments.
highlights: Shipped the v1 alerting pipeline from prototype to GA in nine months
Built the Kubernetes operator that replaced the team's hand-rolled deploy scripts

volunteer.summary: Weekly mentoring sessions for middle-schoolers learning Python and Scratch.

awards.title: Engineering Excellence Award
awards.summary: Annual internal recognition for sustained technical and cultural impact across the engineering org.

publications.name: Designing for the Boring Middle
publications.summary: Essay arguing that most production systems fail in the unremarkable middle of their lifecycle, not at launch or sunset.

projects.name: chrono-drift
projects.description: Small Rust library for detecting clock-drift anomalies in distributed trace data.
highlights: Ships as a single crate with zero runtime dependencies
Used in production at two example-domain companies
